  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "The reported sink is in `dspace-api/src/test/java/org/dspace/app/sherpa/MockSHERPAService.java`, not in deployed production code. `MockSHERPAService.performRequest()` takes the query value, appends `.json`, and calls `getClass().getResourceAsStream(fileName)`, which is a package-relative classpath resource lookup used to load test fixtures. Real user input does reach SHERPA-related entry points in production, for example `ExternalSourcesRestController` request parameters flow through `ExternalSourceRestRepository` and `ExternalDataServiceImpl` into `SHERPAv2*DataProvider.searchExternalDataObjects(...)`, but in production those providers are wired to `org.dspace.app.sherpa.SHERPAService`, whose `performRequest()` performs an HTTP request to SHERPA, not a local resource/file lookup. Only the test Spring configs bind those flows to `MockSHERPAService`. Therefore the CodeQL path depends on an unreachable runtime dispatch from production request handling into a test-only mock implementation. This does not create a real CWE-022 risk in deployed DSpace.",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "A2"
  ],
  "evidence": "Sink code: `MockSHERPAService` is under `src/test/java`; it does `content = getContent(value.concat(\".json\")); if (Objects.isNull(content)) { content = getContent(\"thelancet.json\"); }` and `private InputStream getContent(String fileName) { return getClass().getResourceAsStream(fileName); }` (`dspace-api/src/test/java/org/dspace/app/sherpa/MockSHERPAService.java:57-63,82-83`). The resources it reads are test fixtures in `dspace-api/src/test/resources/org/dspace/app/sherpa/` (`0000-0000.json`, `2731-0582.json`, `plos.json`, `thelancet.json`). Production user input path example: `ExternalSourcesRestController.getExternalSourceEntries(..., @RequestParam(\"query\") String query)` -> `ExternalSourceRestRepository.getExternalSourceEntries(...)` -> `ExternalDataServiceImpl.searchExternalDataObjects(...)` -> `SHERPAv2JournalDataProvider.searchExternalDataObjects(query,...)` -> `sherpaService.performRequest(...)` (`dspace-server-webapp/src/main/java/org/dspace/app/rest/ExternalSourcesRestController.java:56-64`, `dspace-server-webapp/src/main/java/org/dspace/app/rest/repository/ExternalSourceRestRepository.java:70-78`, `dspace-api/src/main/java/org/dspace/external/service/impl/ExternalDataServiceImpl.java:61-68`, `dspace-api/src/main/java/org/dspace/external/provider/impl/SHERPAv2JournalDataProvider.java:115-118`). But production wiring uses the real service: `dspace/config/spring/api/external-services.xml:341-379` injects `new org.dspace.app.sherpa.SHERPAService` into all SHERPA providers, and `dspace-api/src/main/resources/spring/spring-dspace-addon-sherpa-services.xml:22-30` defines the same real bean. Only test wiring injects the mock: `dspace-api/src/test/data/dspaceFolder/config/spring/api/sherpa.xml:36-42` and `dspace-api/src/test/data/dspaceFolder/config/spring/api/external-services.xml:14-35`. The test artifact is also test-scoped only (`pom.xml:1039-1045`).",

  "report": "Rule Name: Arbitrary file access during archive extraction ("Zip Slip")
Rule Description: Extracting files from a malicious ZIP file, or similar type of archive, without validating that the destination file path is within the destination directory can allow an attacker to unexpectedly gain access to resources.
Severity: error
Detailed Message: Unsanitized archive entry, which may contain '..', is used in a [["file system operation"|"relative:///dspace-api/src/test/java/org/dspace/app/sherpa/MockSHERPAService.java:83:47:83:54"]].
Location-file: /dspace-swordv2/src/main/java/org/dspace/sword2/SimpleZipContentIngester.java
Location-start-line: 141
Location-start-col: 36
Location-end-line: 141
Location-end-col: 50",
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "The ZIP entry name at `SimpleZipContentIngester.java:141` is attacker-controlled, so the source is real. However, this is not a real Zip Slip sink. In `unzipToBundle`, the code first normalizes the entry name and rejects absolute or upward-traversing paths (`entryPath.isAbsolute()` or `entryPath.startsWith(\"..\")`). After that, it does not extract to a filesystem path derived from `entryName`. Instead, it reads the entry bytes with `zip.getInputStream(entry)` and stores them via `bitstreamService.create(...)`. That API creates a new DSpace bitstream using a fresh random internal ID, so the server-side storage path is unrelated to the archive entry name. The subsequent `bs.setName(context, entryName)` only saves the ZIP entry name as bitstream metadata, not as a file path. Therefore a malicious entry such as `../../webapps/ROOT/shell.jsp` cannot overwrite or read arbitrary server files through this code path; in the current code it is rejected as a bad request. The CodeQL report also cites a sink in `src/test/java/.../MockSHERPAService.java`, which is unrelated test code and not a production archive-extraction path.",
  "FP_Reason": ["B1"],
  "evidence": "`dspace-swordv2/src/main/java/org/dspace/sword2/SimpleZipContentIngester.java:141-153` shows: `String entryName = entry.getName(); Path entryPath = Paths.get(entryName).normalize(); if (entryPath.isAbsolute() || entryPath.startsWith(\"..\")) throw new SwordError(...); InputStream stream = zip.getInputStream(entry); Bitstream bs = bitstreamService.create(context, target, stream); ... bs.setName(context, entryName);`. `dspace-api/src/main/java/org/dspace/content/Bitstream.java:143-145` shows `setName()` writes metadata via `setMetadataSingleValue(..., \"dc\", \"title\", ..., n)`, so `entryName` becomes metadata only. `dspace-api/src/main/java/org/dspace/storage/bitstore/BitstreamStorageServiceImpl.java:109-123` shows actual storage uses `String id = Utils.generateKey(); bitstream.setInternalId(id); store.put(bitstream, is);`, meaning the filesystem path is based on a generated ID, not the ZIP entry name. `dspace-api/src/main/java/org/dspace/storage/bitstore/DSBitStoreService.java:242-264` further sanitizes/normalizes storage paths and enforces they remain inside the assetstore root. The report's cited sink `dspace-api/src/test/java/org/dspace/app/sherpa/MockSHERPAService.java:83` is only `getResourceAsStream(fileName)` in test code and has no production call path from the SWORD ZIP ingester.",
  "new_reason:": ""
}"
}
